data.title: KLCCP Stapled Group's 1Q profit rises 7%, declares 9.2 sen dividend
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 23): KLCCP Stapled Group (KL:KLCC) said its net profit rose 7.2% in the first quarter from a year earlier, reflecting gains from the acquisition of the remaining 40% stake in Suria KLCC Sdn Bhd.

Its net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) was RM201.48 million versus RM188.03 million previously, the Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas)-owned group said in an exchange filing.

Earnings per share increased to 11.16 sen from 10.42 sen a year ago.

The group, comprising KLCC Property Holdings Bhd and the KLCC Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT), declared a higher interim dividend of 9.2 sen per stapled security, compared with nine sen a year earlier.

KLCC Property Holdings Bhd had in early 2024 bought the remaining 40% equity interest in Suria KLCC, which owns and manages the Suria KLCC mall, for RM1.95 billion.

KLCCP's quarterly revenue slipped 0.5% to RM406.92 million, compared with RM408.90 million in 1QFY2024, mainly due to lower contribution from the hotel segment, as the Mandarin Oriental Kuala Lumpur hotel faced disruptions from ballroom upgrading works and scheduled maintenance during the low travel season.

Revenue for the retail segment, led by Suria KLCC and the retail podium of Menara 3 Petronas, increased to RM143.2 million from RM140.5 million in 1QFY2024, while profit before tax grew 5.3% to RM114.7 million on higher base rents for tenancy renewal and improved occupancy for the retail podium of Menara 3 Petronas.

On a quarter-on-quarter basis, KLCCP’s net profit declined 53.2% from RM430.91 million in 4QFY2024, due to seasonal weakness in the hotel segment and lower contributions from the management services segment.

"Despite the group's softer performance in the first quarter, we remain positive in delivering solid performance underpinned by the resilient foundation of our business segments,” said KLCCP chief executive officer Datuk Mohd Salem Kailany.

The group said it remains cautiously optimistic in delivering a positive performance for FY2025.

Shares of KLCCP closed unchanged at RM8.70 on Friday, giving the group a market capitalisation of RM15.71 billion.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Hibiscus sees one-off tax drag in 3Q; keeps 17% volume growth target amid reserve boost
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 23): Oil and gas producer Hibiscus Petroleum Bhd (KL:HIBISCS) has maintained its full-year sales volumes of 9.1 million barrels of oil equivalent (mmboe), after its January-March volume dipped slightly from the previous immediate quarter.

The group sold 2.1 mmboe in the third quarter ended March 31 (3QFY2025), down from 2.6 mmboe in 2QFY2025.

Its full-year target — 17% higher year-on-year — is short of about 2.7 mmboe, with the total for the nine months ended March 31, 2025 (9MFY2025) nearly 6.4 mmboe, according to Hibiscus' latest and past filings.

The group recently announced a 20-year extension of its production sharing contract in PM3 CAA field northeast of Peninsular Malaysia, estimated to add net proven and probable reserves of 12.6 mmboe, on top of over 133 mmboe of contingent resources.

Market observers were divided on the short-term global oil demand outlook amid tariff-induced global growth concerns this year, which in turn weighed on prices and dragged them to three-year lows.

The weaker prices in the January-March period partly contributed to Hibiscus' normalised net profit of RM51.3 million, down nearly half from RM101.81 million a year earlier. Hibiscus sold 1.8 mmboe in 3QFY2024.

However, the group — the only pure-play upstream producer listed on Bursa Malaysia — turned a net loss of RM115.97 million in the quarter from a net profit of RM101.81 million a year earlier, due to a one-off non-cash deferred tax liability of RM167.3 million, arising from the UK’s Energy Profits Levy (EPL).

Quarterly losses per share stood at 15.4 sen, compared to earnings per share of 12.71 in 3QFY2024.

Nonetheless, the group declared an interim dividend of one sen per share, raising its year-to-date distribution to eight sen per share from six sen last year.

Revenue for the quarter fell 5.1% to RM572.80 million from RM603.51 million, attributed to lower realised oil prices and reduced production volumes.

For 9MFY2025, Hibiscus’ net profit plunged 88% to RM42.89 million or 5.55 sen per share from RM358.44 million or 44.61 sen per share in the same period last year, with revenue down 13.9% to RM1.70 billion from RM1.98 billion. 

Managing director Dr Kenneth Pereira stated, “Despite fluctuating market conditions and a one-off, non-cash deferred tax charge… we delivered strong financial results… and remain firmly on track to achieve our full-year sales volume target.”

The impact of the EPL is expected to reverse by March 2030, the company said.

Hibiscus Petroleum's share price closed up one sen to RM1.45 on Friday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM1.1 billion. The counter is down 26% this year.

data.title: The coming of a perfect financial storm?
data.description:
Read more in the May 26 edition of The Edge Malaysia Weekly. Pick up from the newsstands or subscribe at https://theedgemalaysia.com/subscription

More Issues

data.title: Ho Wah Genting aborts plan to raise RM4m via private placement
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 23): Ho Wah Genting Bhd (KL:HWGB), which is engaged in providing management services with a diverse business portfolio, has aborted its plan to raise RM4.01 million via a private placement of not more than 10% of its issued shares or 20.55 million new shares in the group.

In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, Ho Wah Genting said it has resolved to abort the proposed private placement in view of a resolution for the authority to allot shares not being passed at its annual general meeting held on Friday.

"The company will submit an application to Bursa Securities for the withdrawal of the proposed private placement," it added.

Ho Wah Genting had on April 22 proposed to undertake the corporate exercise, with the aim of using the placement proceeds to repay borrowings, to pay creditors and for working capital purposes.

Ho Wah Genting shares closed unchanged at 22 sen on Friday, giving the group a market capitalisation of RM45 million. The stock has fallen 12% so far this year.

Edited ByKang Siew Li

data.title: IJM wins govt go-ahead for previously scrapped New Pantai Highway extension
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 23): IJM Corp Bhd (KL:IJM) on Friday announced that its unit New Pantai Expressway Sdn Bhd has received the government’s approval to build the 15km fully elevated extension to the New Pantai Highway which was previously scrapped by the government.

The highway will connect the existing Pantai Dalam toll plaza to Jalan Istana Interchange via Jalan Syed Putra.

The government scrapped plans for a proposed extension of the New Pantai Elevated Highway in 2012, but IJM Corp Bhd vowed to appeal the decision.

More to come

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Allianz Malaysia posts 12% increase in 1Q net profit; working towards more profitable product mix
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 23): Composite insurer Allianz Malaysia Bhd (KL:ALLIANZ) said its net income grew 12% in the first quarter from a year earlier, thanks largely to underwriting profit.

Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) was RM211.69 million, or 117.79 sen per share, Allianz Malaysia said in an exchange filing. Year-on-year, gross written premium rose 5.5% to over RM2 billion before expenses and claims.

Allianz Malaysia, the locally listed unit of the German multinational insurer, said it is “actively steering its portfolio towards a more profitable product mix to accelerate sustainable growth and enhance return on equity”.

Operationally, Allianz Malaysia said it is focused on expanding its distribution channels by “driving agency transformation, emphasising high-quality recruitment, agent productivity and retention”.

During the first quarter, Allianz Malaysia said its general insurance growth is driven by the strong performance from its agency and franchise channels though its life insurance segment was affected by the introduction of regulatory interim measures during the period.

Private healthcare costs have skyrocketed amid soaring medical inflation in Malaysia, and spikes in premiums for medical insurance have resulted in public outcry and prompted the central bank to step in.

Bank Negara Malaysia, which also regulates the insurance industry, has asked insurance and takaful operators to spread out the increases in premiums over a minimum of three years for all policyholders affected by the repricing, among other interim measures.

At Allianz Malaysia’s life insurance unit, the annualised new premiums — the full amount of money a policyholder pays for the first year — fell 9.1% when compared to 1QFY2024.

Meanwhile at its general insurance business, the combined ratio — a measure of profitability — fell 1.2 percentage points to 85.8%.

No dividend was declared for the quarter. The company last declared a dividend of 63 sen per share in April for FY2024.

Shares of Allianz Malaysia ended 0.4% lower at RM18.80 ahead of the results announcement. At the last price, the company has a market capitalisation of RM3.4 billion. 

Edited ByJason Ng

data.title: TIME dotCom’s 1Q net profit up 2.1% on retail-driven data demand
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 23): TIME dotCom Bhd (KL:TIMECOM) posted a marginal 2.1% increase in net profit to RM112.99 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) from RM110.67 million a year earlier, on sustained demand for its data and connectivity services, particularly from the retail segment.

Earnings per share stood at 6.11 sen in 1QFY2025, up from 5.99 sen in 1QFY2024, its bourse filing on Friday showed.

Quarterly revenue also rose 2.6% to RM428.59 million from RM417.77 million a year earlier, driven primarily by a 6.1% rise in data services to RM378.34 million, which offset a 22.2% drop in cloud and other services revenue.

No dividend was declared for 1QFY2025.

TIME dotCom reiterated its focus on long-term growth, highlighting continued investments in expanding its network and scaling renewable energy initiatives.

“We’re cautiously optimistic as we navigate 2025. While the industry continues to evolve, we remain focused on what matters most — delivering reliable services, enhancing the customer experience and strengthening our core capabilities,” said TIME dotCom commander-in-chief Afzal Abdul Rahim.

Looking ahead, the group said it will continue to pursue operational efficiencies and remain agile in navigating an ever-changing domestic and global landscape, proactively managing emerging risks and opportunities.

Shares in TIME dotCom closed down nine sen or 1.71% to RM5.18 on Friday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM9.58 billion. The stock has risen over 9% year to date.

Edited ByKang Siew Li

data.title: BofA’s Hartnett says buy the dip in Treasuries as yields top 5%
data.description:
(May 23): Investors should buy the sell-off in long-dated Treasuries as the government is likely to heed warnings from bond vigilantes to bring its debt under control, according to Bank of America Corp’s Michael Hartnett.

The 30-year Treasury note is at a “great entry point” with the yield above 5%, the strategist wrote. Bond investors are “incentivised to punish the unambiguously unsustainable path of debt and deficit,” he added.

US bond yields have surged this week as President Donald Trump’s tax cut plan has ignited concerns that it would add trillions of dollars in coming years to already bulging budget deficits, at a time when investor appetite is waning for US assets across the globe. Sentiment toward Treasuries has also taken a hit since Moody’s Ratings stripped the US of its top credit grade late last week.

The 30-year yield rose to as high as 5.15% on Thursday, just shy of a two-decade high. Long-dated bonds in Japan, Germany, Australia and the UK have also been under pressure, while US equities and the dollar have retreated.

Hartnett has recommended bonds over equities this year. The strategist said in the note dated Thursday that Treasuries are now reflecting the drivers of a bear market, with 10-year annualised returns from long-term government bonds falling to a record low of -1.3% in January.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: NEM Rakyat quota increased by 100MW to 600MW — ministry
data.description:
PUTRAJAYA (May 23): The quota for the Net Energy Metering (NEM) programme under the domestic category, known as NEM Rakyat, has been increased by an additional 100 megawatts (MW), bringing the total quota allocated since the programme's inception in 2011 to 600MW.

In a statement on Friday, the Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (Petra) said the additional quota is now open to all domestic users under Tenaga Nasional Bhd or TNB (KL:TENAGA) until June 30, or until the quota is fully subscribed — whichever comes first.

Petra said the move follows an overwhelming response from the public, which led to the exhaustion of the existing NEM Rakyat quota.

“In light of the strong interest, Petra has decided to expand the NEM Rakyat quota to enable more domestic users to harness their rooftop spaces for the installation of solar photovoltaic systems,” the statement read.

The latest guidelines for the NEM Rakyat programme can be accessed via the Energy Commission’s website at www.st.gov.my, while further details of the application process are available on the Sustainable Energy Development Authority (Seda) Malaysia’s website at www.seda.gov.my.

The ministry also said that it, together with the Energy Commission as the regulatory authority for the electricity supply industry, is currently reviewing new mechanisms and implementation methods for rooftop solar initiatives.

The aim is to ensure that upcoming renewable energy initiatives are more inclusive, equitable and beneficial to all electricity consumers.

“As the lead agency driving the national energy transition agenda, Petra remains committed to enhancing and expanding the implementation of rooftop solar initiatives as part of a broader effort to support Malaysia’s energy transition aspirations,” the ministry said. 

It also reaffirmed Malaysia’s goal of achieving 70% renewable energy capacity in the national electricity supply mix by 2050 — a key pillar in the country’s sustainable development agenda and a testament to its commitment to environmental sustainability and energy resource security.

Read also:
Residential rooftop solar quota under Net Energy Metering fully taken up

Uploaded by Tham Yek Lee

data.title: Eco World International inks first local deal after shift to Malaysian market
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 23): Eco World International Bhd (KL:EWINT) has teamed up with JLand Group (JLG), part of Johor Corporation, for its first project in Malaysia.

They will explore real estate opportunities in both Malaysia and Australia, as Eco World International (EWI) marks its entry into the Malaysian market.

In a statement on Friday, EWI said the non-binding deal executed via JLG Investment Holdings sets the stage for both sides to explore promising projects and find the best ways to work together on land owned or identified by either party.

JLG has expressed interest in EWI’s residential site at Macquarie Park, Australia — a prime location just 18km from Sydney’s central business district.

Meanwhile, EWI is considering involvement in a proposed 300-acre industrial development within Ibrahim Technopolis (IBTEC), JLG’s flagship integrated township in Sedenak, Johor.

Situated within the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), IBTEC has secured over RM34 billion in committed investments.

If the deal moves forward, it will be EWI’s first real estate project in Malaysia and support JLG’s regional growth plans.

Datuk Sr Akmal Ahmad, group managing director of JLand Group emphasised that this potential partnership reflects the group’s commitment to scale with the right collaborators who share its long-term vision, values and execution discipline.

“Macquarie Park is more than just access to a mature market — it presents an opportunity for JLG to introduce its integrated, human-centric development approach in a new setting,” said Akmal.

EWI CEO Datuk Teow Leong Seng said he is excited about the company’s possible role in IBTEC, a top smart industrial zone in Malaysia. He also noted that EWI has five unlaunched sites in the UK and Australia, where housing shortages have boosted rental demand.

“As central banks globally begin to normalise interest rates, investors’ yield expectations should moderate and homebuying activity is also expected to recover eventually.

“Depending on market conditions and project feasibility, these sites could be developed as build-to-sell, build-to-rent, or student housing to align with current market needs,” he said.

“With JLG’s interest in Macquarie Park, we hope to bring our projects to market sooner to meet this anticipated demand,” he added.

In late April, EWI announced it would expand into the Malaysian property market, ending its long-standing agreement with Eco World Development Group (KL:ECOWLD) that limited it to overseas projects.

EWI's shares closed one sen lower to 34 sen on Friday for a market value of RM816 million. The stock is up 25.9% so far this year.

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: SAM Engineering ends FY2025 on weaker note, pays 2.8 sen dividend
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 23): SAM Engineering & Equipment (M) Bhd (KL:SAM), which manufactures production equipment and aerospace products, saw its net profit for its fourth financial quarter ended March 31, 2025 (4QFY2025) slip 2% to RM25.59 million from RM26.1 million a year earlier, on lower profit contributions from the equipment and aerospace segments.

This resulted in a lower earnings per share of 3.78 sen for 4QFY2025 compared with 4.35 sen for 4QFY2024.

Revenue for the quarter was flattish at RM370.09 million from RM373.62 million in 4QFY2024, on lower revenue from its equipment segment due to lower demand from data storage customers and unfavourable foreign exchange translation.

The group also declared a first interim dividend of 2.8 sen per share for the financial year ended March 31, 2025 (FY2025), payable on Aug 8.

In a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Friday, SAM Engineering said the lower profit for 4QFY2025 from the equipment segment was mainly due to lower sales and under-recovery of cost due to low capacity utilisation offset by lower interest expense.

For the aerospace segment, the lower profit was mainly due to lower sales, but this was offset by a positive contribution from its subsidiary Aviatron (M) Sdn Bhd — a manufacturer of aircraft structure parts and precision engineering components.

For FY2025, SAM Engineering posted a 15.3% decline in net profit to RM91.92 million from RM108.58 million in FY2024, while revenue slipped 1.1% to RM1.48 billion from RM1.5 billion.

Looking ahead, SAM Engineering said the current economic outlook is uncertain, but in the longer term it remains cautiously optimistic in respect of the aerospace and semiconductor industries.

SAM Engineering shares closed up 14 sen or 3.64% at RM3.99 on Friday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM2.7 billion. The stock has fallen 9.73% so far this year.

Edited ByKang Siew Li

data.title: InNature seeks to acquire The Body Shop in Singapore

data.title: Sim: Several policies in place to address imbalance between high-value jobs and graduates
data.description:
KLANG (May 23): To overcome the imbalance between the number of high-value jobs offered in the labour market and the number of graduates from higher learning institutions as well as technical and vocational education and training (TVET), the government has launched several policies to address the matter, according to Human Resources Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong.

The minister said among the policies in question are the Madani Economy framework and progressive salary policy, which are among the efforts made by the government across ministries to ensure that every graduate or worker receives a dignified remuneration.

"We have about 300,000 university graduates yearly, plus 100,000 TVET graduates, making a total of 400,000 Malaysians with higher education qualifications, but every year we are only able to generate 50,000 high-value and highly skilled jobs," he said.

He said this when met after the 2025 Trade Union Affairs Programme (PHEKS) Central Zone assistance handover ceremony here on Friday.

Sim also encouraged workers to join trade unions, so that suitable salaries or remuneration could be negotiated well with employers.

On Thursday, the media reported that more than 65% of degree holders in the country earn a monthly salary of below RM3,000, thus earning an "enough to eat" income with no room to save, invest or increase their social mobility.

In addition, more than 70% of graduates in the country were found to be forced to work in the semi-skilled and unskilled sectors.

The findings were obtained through a study titled The 'Gaji Cukup Makan' Economy: When Higher Education Becomes an Economic Risk, conducted under the Malaysia Labour Market Insight Series.

Uploaded by Tham Yek Lee

data.title: Govt backed-Ekuinas names Aliff Omar as new CEO
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 23): Ekuiti Nasional Bhd (Ekuinas), a state-owned Bumiputera-focused investment firm, has appointed Aliff Omar Mohamad Omar as its chief executive officer (CEO), effective Friday.

Aliff served as interim CEO of Ekuinas from April 1 this year, following the resignation of Datuk Syed Yasir Arafat Syed Abd Kadir, who had helmed the chief post for nine years. The statement from Ekuinas confirms The Edge’s report on March 26, on the change in leadership at Ekuinas.

Aliff, with Ekuinas since 2017, has over 16 years of experience in investment banking and advisory, having worked at UBS AG, CIMB Investment Bank, and Maybank Investment Bank.

“The board is confident that Aliff’s leadership will strengthen Ekuinas’ position in the Malaysian private capital ecosystem, and take the organisation into its next phase of strategic impact,” Ekuinas chairman Tan Sri Shahril Ridza Ridzuan said in a statement.

Aliff’s appointment comes as Ekuinas moves beyond traditional private equity, adding private credit in late 2024.

“This evolution reflects our ambition to become a more holistic private capital investor — one that is better-equipped to support the diverse and dynamic needs of Malaysian businesses across various stages of growth,” Aliff said.

“Our mission remains clear as we create meaningful, measurable value for our stakeholders and contribute to a more inclusive and resilient economy,” he added.

Read also:
Ekuinas announces CEO Syed Yasir Arafat to step down on March 31
Ekuinas to be under PNB’s purview, say sources
Change of guard at Ekuinas — sources

 

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Taiwan's Foxconn among potential bidders for Singaporean chip assembly firm UTAC in US$3b deal, Reuters reports
data.description:
HONG KONG (May 23): Taiwan's Hon Hai Precision Industry Co Ltd is among potential bidders for the Singaporean semiconductor assembly and testing business UTAC Holdings in a deal that could value the company at about US$3 billion (RM12.71 billion), two people with knowledge of the matter said.

UTAC's owner, Beijing-based private equity firm Wise Road Capital, has hired Jefferies to run a sale process, and is expecting to field non-binding bids by end of this month, the sources said.

Both the sources declined to be named as the information was confidential.

Hon Hai, commonly known as Foxconn, and Jefferies declined to comment, while UTAC and Wise Road did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Global chip manufacturing has been beset by concerns over national security and technological competition in recent years, particularly between the US and China.

Historically, semiconductors have relied on a very global manufacturing supply chain, but the US has made moves to curb China's access to advanced chips and high-end manufacturing tools, citing risks that these would benefit China's military, which Beijing denies.

The sources said that UTAC is likely to attract interest from financial and strategic bidders that are not from the US due to its China presence.

Foxconn, a major supplier to Apple and the world's largest contract electronics maker, has expanded into making semiconductors in recent years as part of its long-term growth strategy, its website says.

Founded in 1997 in Singapore, UTAC provides assembly and test services for semiconductor chips with various end uses, including consumer, computing and security devices and medical applications.

Outside Singapore, it also has production facilities in Thailand, China and Indonesia and with a global sales network that covers the US, Europe and Asia, according to the company website.

Its customers are primarily "fabless" companies — meaning those that outsource fabrication — as well as integrated device manufacturers and wafer foundries.

UTAC does not disclose its financial performance but its estimated annual earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation are about US$300 million, said the sources.

Uploaded by Tham Yek Lee

data.title: Signature Alliance Group’s IPO sees muted demand, gets just enough applications to fill offering
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Signature Alliance Group Bhd received just enough applications from retail investors to cover the shares offered for its listing on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia.

Individual public investors applied for a total of 56.07 million new shares for 50 million new shares available for subscription under the initial public offering (IPO), Signature Alliance said in a statement. That translates into a subscription rate of 1.12 times or oversubscription of just 0.12 times.

There was enough demand for both Bumiputera and non-Bumiputera portions while shares set aside for eligible persons were subscribed. A private placement was also fully taken up.

Signature Alliance is scheduled to list on the ACE Market on June 5, 2025. 

The company houses interior fit-out and renovation subsidiaries spun off from Signature International Bhd (KL:SIGN) mostly known for its kitchen cabinets. Chin Hin Group Bhd (KL:CHINHIN) controls Signature International with a 71.72% stake.

The IPO, priced at 62 sen apiece, raised RM161.2 million from issue of new shares. There is no sale of existing shares, meaning that its current shareholders are not cashing out during the IPO.

As investors turned increasingly selective at a time of market uncertainties, ACE Market IPOs have struggled with weakened demand. PEOPLElogy Bhd (KL:PEOPLE), listed on May 20, ended the streak of first-day flops on the ACE Market.

Before PEOPLElogy’s debut, however, all nine companies listed since March have ended their first day of trading underwater.

More than half of the proceeds have been earmarked for land acquisition and construction of a 50,000-square-feet production facility adjacent to its new headquarters in Selangor. Signature Alliance will also use part of the proceeds to expand its Penang office and open a new branch in Johor.

The rest will go towards repayment of bank loans for machinery and equipment, as working capital, and to cover listing expenses.

Upon listing, Signature Alliance is expected to have a market capitalisation of RM620 million.

M&A Securities is the adviser, sponsor, managing underwriter, joint underwriter and joint placement agent for the IPO. Affin Hwang Investment Bank is also an underwriter and placement agent.

Read also:
Signature Alliance Group's ACE Market IPO aims to raise RM161.2 mil at 62 sen per share

Edited ByJason Ng

data.title: EU to delay capital rules for bank trading desks by one year
data.description:
(May 23): The European Union (EU) has decided to delay the application of tougher capital requirements for banks’ trading businesses as the bloc is concerned about the industry’s competitiveness.

The regulation known as Fundamental Review of the Trading Book (FRTB) will be pushed back by one year to the start of 2027, a European Commission spokesperson said in response to questions from Bloomberg. It’s the second such delay.

The EU decision comes amid rising pushback from businesses and national governments across the bloc against what they see as excessive red tape, especially in financial services. France, which is home to some of Europe’s biggest banks, has emerged as a strong proponent of easing financial regulation to boost growth.

Europe’s original plan to implement the FRTB regulations this year was derailed after the US failed to agree on its own version of a wider package of capital rules, which is known as the Basel endgame in industry parlance. Washington’s appetite for introducing the entire package has been thrown into further doubt by US President Donald Trump’s administration, which has signalled a broad deregulation agenda.

Avoid disadvantage

The commission had previously postponed FRTB implementation in an effort to avoid putting lenders such as Deutsche Bank AG and BNP Paribas SA at a disadvantage to their US competitors. 

Deutsche Bank has estimated that the introduction of the FRTB will increase its risk-weighted assets — which determine how much capital it needs to hold as buffer — by about €7.5 billion (US$8.5 billion or RM36.2 billion). Meanwhile, Societe Generale SA has said some decisions on excess capital distribution depend on whether the regulation will be delayed.

Financial Services Commissioner Maria Luis Albuquerque indicated at a meeting with European finance ministers earlier this month that the commission will shortly adopt a new delegated act in this area, the spokesperson said. That was based on the analysis of the responses to a consultation and its own technical assessment.

The commission asked banks and other market participants whether it should implement the standards as planned, delay them by a year or make changes for as long as three years. Potential concessions on the trading rules included alleviating data requirements, spacing out reports and other issues.

European banking regulations allow the commission “to postpone the entry into force of the FRTB or to change it, for a time limited period, by way of a delegated act”, the spokesperson said.

Uploaded by Tham Yek Lee

data.title: Eco-Shop Marketing ends Main Market debut with 6% gain
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 23): Eco-Shop Marketing Bhd (KL:ECOSHOP) rose 6% on its first day on the Main Market of Bursa Malaysia, after the dollar-store retail chain lowered the offering price of its shares.

Shares of Eco-Shop opened at RM1.25 on Friday, versus its initial public offering (IPO) price of RM1.13 per share. The stock eased but remained above water and ended Friday at RM1.20, after more than 200 million shares exchanged hands.

A day before the listing, Nomura and UOB Kay Hian initiated coverage on Eco-Shop with “buy” calls and target prices of RM1.35 to RM1.45. 

The gain on Friday also injects more hope into Bursa Malaysia, which has recently suffered a slew of first-day listing flops and weakened investors demand. 

Eco-Shop’s own IPO saw applications from retail investors barely covering the shares allocated, though the institutional tranche was fully taken up. The company then cut its final IPO price by about 7% ahead of the listing.

Gross proceeds totalled RM974 million, the largest since minimart operator 99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Bhd’s IPO (KL:99SMART) raised RM2.36 billion in August 2024.

Before launching the IPO, Eco-Shop signed up 10 cornerstone investors — including AHAM Asset Management, Areca Capital Sdn Bhd, and Eastspring Investments Bhd — that collectively took up more than 90% of the institutional offering.

Cornerstone investors — typically large institutional investors such as pension funds and insurers — commit to take up a portion of IPO shares before they are marketed to other retail and institutional investors. Their presence helps to build up other investors’ confidence for the shares during the IPO.

The IPO raised RM392 million for Eco-Shop and another RM582 million for its selling shareholders that include founder and managing director Datuk Seri Lee Kar Whatt, as well as Creador — a private equity firm.

Creador is also the same firm that backed home improvement retailer Mr DIY Group (M) Bhd (KL:MRDIY).

Eco-Shop operates more than 350 stores nationwide, selling over 10,000 products that include third-party brands, ranging from daily essentials to household items, mostly priced at RM2.60.

Bulk of the proceeds from the IPO has been earmarked for expansion of its distribution centres and retail network. The company will also set aside some funds to repay bank loans, and upgrade its IT systems.

Maybank Investment Bank is acting as principal adviser, joint global coordinator, joint bookrunner and sole underwriter for the IPO. UBS Securities Malaysia and UBS AG Singapore are also joint global coordinators and bookrunners, while RHB Investment Bank joins as a joint bookrunner.

Read also:
Eco-Shop's above-market valuation backed by expansion potential, dollar store appeal — analysts 

Edited ByJason Ng

data.title: BYD’s HK shares hit record premium over onshore stock
data.description:
(May 23): BYD Co’s shares rose to a record high in Hong Kong, expanding their premium over those trading on the mainland to the highest level ever.

The shares gained as much as 4.4% in the financial hub, buoyed by positive sentiment surrounding Contemporary Amperex Technology Co’s blockbuster debut. The stock is now priced at more than a 5% premium over the Shenzhen-listed shares, after adjusting for currency conversion, according to Bloomberg-compiled data.

Advances in the electric carmaker’s Hong Kong shares underscore growing conviction among foreign investors over the company’s prospects, making them a rare standout among dual-listed stocks. Shares listed on the mainland currently trade at around 33% premium to their Hong Kong counterparts, according to the Hang Seng Stock Connect China AH Premium Index.

Mainland shares’ premium over Hong Kong will remain on an aggregate level, but certain stocks such as BYD and China Merchants Bank Co that are considered “quality core holdings for foreign investors” have shown discounts, UBS AG strategist James Wang wrote in a note. That’s also helped by better liquidity offshore, they added.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Chinese firms’ stellar Hong Kong debuts spur hopes of valuation shift
data.description:
(May 23): The robust performance of two major Hong Kong stock listings this week has raised hopes that a small group of elite Chinese companies may start driving a shift to end the city’s historical discount to mainland markets. 

The latest addition to the cohort is Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals Co, China’s largest drugmaker by market value, which surged as much as 37% in its trading debut Friday in the Asian financial hub after raising HK$9.9 billion (US$1.3 billion or RM5.4 billion). The company’s H-shares briefly commanded a 0.3% premium over its Shanghai-listed A-shares, before reverting to a discount of 3.2%.

This comes after battery giant Contemporary Amperex Technology Co’s (CATL) US$5.2 billion’s initial public offering in the city, the world’s biggest listing this year. Its shares have soared 22% since its Tuesday debut and are around 10% pricier than its Shenzhen-listed stock.

Among the nearly 150 firms that have listings in both Hong Kong and mainland China, currently only CATL, BYD Co, and China Merchants Bank Co are trading at a premium in Hong Kong, according to Bloomberg-compiled data.

Chinese companies’ Hong Kong-listed stocks have for decades suffered deep discounts to their onshore peers due to a confluence of factors, including poorer liquidity and geopolitical headwinds that often curb investor enthusiasm in the city. Meanwhile, long-term investors buying H-shares via a trading link are subject to a higher dividend tax, which also caps the stocks’ valuations.

The strong showing of CATL and Hengrui comes on the back of a broader revival of Chinese equities in recent weeks, buoyed a trade truce between the US and China and fresh steps taken by Beijing to spur economic growth. Their stellar listing debuts are rekindling hopes that a small but growing number of Chinese firms with global ambitions may attract enough demand to defy the long-standing valuation gap. 

“Many Chinese firms that are globally competitive are increasingly being recognised for their worth, meaning companies like CATL won’t see an H-share discount, but an A-share one,” said Zhai Jingyong, the managing director of Banyan Investment Management Co.

To be sure, A-shares as a whole still trade at a premium of 32% to H-shares, compared with a low of 28% in March, according to the Hang Seng Stock Connect China AH Premium Index. Among the dual-listed stocks, 42 names carry a discount of 50% or more in Hong Kong, Bloomberg-compiled data showed. 

“While some of the gains in CATL and Hengrui may be due to a relatively small float in Hong Kong, there is potential to attract even more foreign capital as they get included in the HSI and MSCI gauges,” said Alvin Ngan, an analyst at Zhongtai Financial International Ltd. Their performance may spark more demand for coming Hong Kong listings, he added. 

Read also:
Dimon says JPMorgan was right to do CATL deal despite US opposition

Uploaded by Tham Yek Lee
